mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and pushing S&P 500 returns positive over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0, or the signal's predictive power decays to zero after 2015.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with declining housing starts signals a structural shift from construction-led to manufacturing-led growth, which historically precedes equity outperformance over the next 60 trading days as corporate capex cycles turn positive.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of historical instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, rising short-term rates compress equity multiples, but if CPI inflation simultaneously exceeds 3.0 percent, real rates remain negative, sustaining positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as nominal earnings outpace discount rate hikes.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns turn negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the real rate spread ceases to predict equity direction after 2013.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential quarterly increase in real GDP growth expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically driving positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days as analysts revise consensus estimates upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth turns positive but 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of observations, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index breaks above its 12-month moving average, supply chain bottlenecks ease and capacity utilization rises, leading to margin expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in over 55% of threshold breaches, or the signal loses predictive power during supply-side shocks like oil price spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing permits indicates broad-based consumer confidence and durable goods demand, which historically triggers positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as cyclical sectors outperform.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the cross-product term's t-statistic falls below 1.5 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle environment where payroll employment growth exceeds 200,000 per month, wage-push inflation forces the Federal Reserve to pause rate cuts, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the negative predictive relationship reverses during recessionary labor market softening.
